title: Persona Vectors — Monitoring and Controlling Character Traits in Language Models

absences.notes: Interpretability methodology present but applied to safety-relevant behavioural traits (evil, sycophancy, hallucination), not welfare-relevant states. Decision rule #6 not met — representations studied are not framed as "experiential or welfare-relevant."
No model self-report methodology. LLM-judge evaluation used instead.
No sentience vocabulary. "Functional emotions" not invoked. Emotion-adjacent traits (optimism, humor) mentioned only in appendices as methodology-generalisation examples.
Traits described behaviourally; no attribution of goals/stakes/interests.
"Moral" vocabulary appears only in trait descriptions (evil system prompts, SAE feature labels) — not engaging with AI moral status.
Persona/character vocabulary throughout, but treated as activation-space patterns. Codebook counter-example match: character/personality without what-kind-of-entity engagement.
All wellbeing/welfare references are to human wellbeing.
Training-induced personality shifts discussed extensively but strictly in safety/alignment framing. No authenticity, welfare, or ethics-of-training-modification engagement.
Persona treated as activation-space direction, not as minded property. "Simulated 'Assistant' persona" (scare quotes) signals functional framing.
No reflexive engagement with whether trait-attribution is anthropomorphic.
Emotion-adjacent traits (sycophancy, optimism, humor) mentioned as methodology-generalisation examples without emotional-design-calibration engagement.
